Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

spike(MT-037): tvOS YouTube playback — NO-GO (#98)

WebKit framework is not available on tvOS, meaning WKWebView and the
YouTube IFrame Player API cannot be used on Apple TV. This blocks the
original tvOS playback approach.

Key findings:
- WebKit absent from tvOS SDK (verified tvOS 26.2)
- youtube-ios-player-helper also depends on WKWebView → blocked
- Only viable option: deep-link to native YouTube app (loses safety controls)
- Documented alternatives and recommendation in docs/spikes/

opens their detail view.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

revert: restore receive polling, drop subscribeReceive

subscribeReceive causes signal-cli to throw InvalidMessageStructureException
for Note-to-Self sync messages, silently discarding them. The older
receive-polling approach returns buffered envelopes including sync messages
without this validation error, so Note-to-Self works correctly.

Restores _check_messages() / interval polling, removes _subscribe_and_listen.
Also restores manual "check now" poll and poll-interval status line.

commit.message:
Phase 3 — ax controls (#22)

* Split countdown into COUNTDOWN.md, rename to README.md, add Setup event (#21)

* Phase 3 becomes ax controls — not Stop-specific, any event can query attestations

* Ax controls on Stop — clippy-reminder queries attestation trail and blocks when .rs edits are newer than last cargo clippy run

* File-path controls, UserPromptSubmit reminders, SessionStart arch, once-per-session patterns

Graunde now handles non-Bash tool events — Edit/Write/Read on file paths matching
scope patterns get context injected (replaces remind-web-docs.sh). Stop responses use
continue+systemMessage so Claude keeps going. Clippy and keyword reminders fire once
per session via attestation check/write.

* Keyword reminders (QNTX, timer), install-after-test control, parse.d extraction, SessionStart source awareness

* Add CLAUDE.md with pointers to key project files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* PostToolUse response capture, run_in_background amendment, once-per-session msg controls

Attestations now include stdout/stderr/filePath/success from tool responses.
PreToolUse controls can set run_in_background and timeout on Bash commands.
Msg-only controls deduplicate per session via attestation check.
CI nudge after git push in graunde (once per session, prefix-matched).

* ci-check control, fix sync-main splitting, remove pull-checkpoint

pull-checkpoint belongs in PostToolUse (conflicts surface in output, not before).
sync-main matches git checkout main (first segment) instead of unsplittable && chain.
gh run list auto-approves everywhere via ci-check control.

* Deferred message queue — non-blocking CI nudge via attestation-backed delayed delivery

PostToolUse writes a deferred attestation on git push instead of blocking with sleep.
PreToolUse checks for pending messages on every tool call and delivers when the delay elapses.
General-purpose mechanism — any hook event can schedule a message for future delivery.

* Deliver deferred messages on Stop instead of PreToolUse

Stop blocks Claude with decision:block + reason when a deferred message is ready.
Removed once-per-session guard — every git push schedules a CI check.
Per-instance delivery tracking via timestamp comparison, not name-only dedup.

* Fix CI nudge for compound push commands — contains instead of prefix match

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* hasSegment for compound command matching, emit allow decision on repeat msg-only fires

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update Phase 3 docs — deferred queue, hasSegment, adaptive delay (#33)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix build: add ceell sources to app target instead of separate library

zephyr_library() in subdirectories creates libraries that register with
ZEPHYR_LIBS, but the link step is configured during find_package(Zephyr)
before add_subdirectory(ceell) runs. The libraries get built but aren't
linked.

Fix: add all ceell sources directly to the app target using
target_sources(app PRIVATE ...) with absolute paths. This is the
standard pattern for application-level code in Zephyr — the app target
is always in the link group.

commit.message:
Replace minimal IDL stub with complete IDL in SDK

The SDK was using a minimal IDL stub with empty arrays for instructions,
accounts, and types, which meant Anchor could not properly deserialize
accounts or build instructions. This replaces it with a full IDL manually
constructed from programs/market-engine/src/lib.rs that accurately
reflects all 6 instructions (initialize, create_market, stake, unstake,
settle_market, claim_rewards), 3 account types (MarketConfig, Market,
UserPosition), the Outcome enum, 5 events, and 9 error codes.

The IDL is now in its own module (sdk/src/idl.ts) and exported from the
SDK so consumers can also use it directly.

fix(ci): remove spn-client path dependency entirely

The spn-client path dependency was causing CI failures because the
supernovae-cli repo doesn't exist in the CI environment.

Changes:
- Remove spn-client dependency from Cargo.toml (commented for docs)
- Remove spn-daemon feature (no longer needed)
- Simplify secrets.rs to only use keyring/env fallback
- Update Cargo.lock accordingly

The spn daemon integration can be re-enabled locally by uncommenting
the dependency and re-adding the feature when needed.

commit.message:
fix(web): auth redirect loop and hooks violation on all protected pages

Root cause: useAuth() initializes with authenticated=false before useEffect
reads localStorage. Pages checked !authenticated on first render → immediate
redirect to /login even when user was authenticated.

Secondary bug: pontos, beneficios, indicacoes, vouchers, avaliacoes pages
called useApi hooks AFTER conditional return, violating React Rules of Hooks
→ client-side crash.

Fix: All 9 protected pages now wait for authLoading before checking auth.
All hooks moved before any conditional returns. Header shows placeholder
while auth loading to prevent "Entrar" flash.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement sendNotification() for p2p friend request approvals (WOP-1666) (#29)

* feat: implement sendNotification() for p2p friend request approvals (WOP-1666)

- Add src/notification.ts with sendFriendRequestNotification() and handleOwnerReply()
- Wire initNotification() and handleOwnerReply() callback into initMessageHandler()
- Expose sendFriendRequestNotification on the whatsapp extension
- Owner replies ACCEPT/DENY via WhatsApp DM to approve/deny friend requests
- Fix pre-existing biome formatting issues across src/ to pass quality gate
- Fix pre-existing zod v4 z.record() call (requires key schema as first arg)
- Add tests/notification.test.ts with 11 passing tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address code review issues from PR #29

- notification.ts: key pending requests by signature instead of requestFrom
  to prevent collisions when the same sender has multiple concurrent requests
- notification.ts: call cleanupExpiredNotifications() before adding a new
  pending request to bound memory growth in long-running processes
- notification.ts: fix DENY error handling — send error message to owner on
  p2p failure instead of a misleading "denied" confirmation; send fallback
  "denied" only when p2p extension is unavailable
- message-handler.ts: wrap _handleOwnerReply() in try/catch so errors in the
  notification handler don't crash the message processing pipeline
- tests: add coverage for ACCEPT error path, DENY error path, and DENY
  fallback when p2p extension is unavailable (now 14 notification tests)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: callback ordering, periodic cleanup wiring (WOP-1666)

- Move pendingRequests.delete to finally block so the entry is always
  cleaned up after the p2p callback attempt, not before — prevents
  silent discard when acceptFriendRequest/denyFriendRequest throws
- Add startNotificationCleanup/stopNotificationCleanup to notification.ts
  and wire them in index.ts init/shutdown so expired entries are pruned
  every 60s regardless of whether new requests arrive
- Add tests covering delete-ordering guarantee and periodic cleanup interval

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address coderabbitai review issues (PR #29)

- connection.ts: add .catch() to handleIncomingMessage call to avoid
  unhandled promise rejections on message processing failures
- credentials.ts: wrap maybeRunMigration pre-check storage.get in
  try/catch so transient backend errors skip migration gracefully
- message-handler.ts: always reset _handleOwnerReply (use no-op when
  no handler provided) to prevent stale handler from prior init call
- messaging.ts: fix chunkMessage to split overlong sentences (> maxLength)
  into fixed-size slices so no chunk ever exceeds maxLength
- notification.ts: fix docstring to say "oldest" instead of "most-recent"
  to match the < comparator that selects oldest pending request

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address CodeRabbit review issues (WOP-1666)

- connection.ts: add .catch() to handleIncomingMessage() call to prevent unhandled promise rejections
- credentials.ts: wrap storage.get() in try/catch in maybeRunMigration() to handle transient backend errors gracefully
- message-handler.ts: always reset _handleOwnerReply (use ?? no-op) so re-init without handler clears stale reference
- messaging.ts: fix chunkMessage() to hard-split overlong sentences so no chunk exceeds maxLength
- notification.ts: fix docstring mismatch — "most-recent" → "oldest" to match oldest-first selection logic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address automated code review issues (PR #29)

- connection.ts: catch unhandled promise rejections from handleIncomingMessage in messages.upsert listener (coderabbit critical)
- credentials.ts: wrap storage.get() in try/catch in maybeRunMigration to avoid hard failure on transient backend errors (coderabbit major)
- message-handler.ts: always reset _handleOwnerReply to no-op when not provided, preventing stale handler from persisting across re-initializations (coderabbit minor)
- messaging.ts: fix chunkMessage to split sentences longer than maxLength into subchunks, preventing oversized chunks from failing to send (coderabbit major)
- notification.ts: correct docstring from "most-recent" to "oldest" (comparator selects oldest); fix misleading "Accepted/denied" confirmations when p2p extension is unavailable (qodo correctness)
- whatsapp-extension.ts: chain .split(":")[0].split("@")[0] to correctly strip device suffix from Baileys JID phoneNumber (macroscope bug)
- tests/notification.test.ts: tighten fallback assertions to verify "not available" message content

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address coderabbitai review issues (WOP-1666)

- connection.ts: add .catch() to handleIncomingMessage() to prevent
  unhandled promise rejections in the messages.upsert listener
- credentials.ts: wrap storage.get() in try/catch in maybeRunMigration()
  so transient backend errors don't abort initialization
- message-handler.ts: always reset _handleOwnerReply (use no-op default
  when handleOwnerReply not provided, preventing stale handler reuse)
- messaging.ts: fix chunkMessage() to split sentences longer than
  maxLength so no chunk ever exceeds the limit
- notification.ts: fix docstring — says "oldest" to match < comparator

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: ownerJid guard, sender validation, TTL cleanup (WOP-1666)

Throw when owner JID is not configured instead of silently returning
false, preventing notification from being silently dropped when the
owner is unset.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address automated reviewer issues (PR #29)

- connection.ts: log credential-save failures instead of swallowing them silently
- credentials.ts: call maybeRestoreCredsFromBackup before reading creds.json in migration path
- index.ts: hasCredentials in WebMCP now returns socket !== null when using Storage API, fixing false "awaiting_scan" status
- message-handler.ts: remove duplicate injectMessage() call in catch block; add media-file cleanup in the text+media path via finally block
- notification.ts: sanitize channelName with newline stripping to prevent message injection; move pendingRequests.delete() before async p2p calls (delete-then-process prevents concurrent duplicate processing)

commit.message:
feat: Broaden .gitignore, remove scaffolding, add new API routes and session auth

- Replace narrow .env patterns with .env* glob + !.env.example allowlist (defense-in-depth for .env.production)
- Remove tracked scaffolding (.gitkeep, boilerplate READMEs, default Next.js SVGs, soc-auth.png)
- Add session-based auth (login/logout/session API routes, session.ts, middleware rewrite)
- Add new API routes: events/search, events/details, channels/live, channels/fieldset-discover,
  channels/fieldset-update, clients/compare, connectors/agent-fields, auth/*
- Add GWT-RPC query client, server cache utility, matrix background component
- Expand arcsight-channel-client with three-layer device enrichment, channel metadata cache,
  EventService, ReportService, and GroupService integration
- Update dashboard pages (channels, clients, devices) and layout
- Add .env.example template and warm-up script

- Platform-specific optimization (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Google AI)
